posts.text: I'm trying to render a 3D model in OpenGL but the texture mapping keeps coming out wrong. Has anyone dealt with UV coordinates being flipped on import?
The Maple Leafs had another rough night. Their defence simply cannot handle fast breakaways and it's been a problem all season.
My doctor mentioned metformin as a first-line treatment for type 2 diabetes. What are the common side effects people actually experience day to day?
The Second Amendment is clear. Any attempt to regulate magazine capacity is an unconstitutional infringement on the right to bear arms.
The ontological argument for God's existence fails because it conflates existence as a predicate with existence as a property. Kant demolished this centuries ago.
Just replaced the timing belt on my '94 Honda Accord. Took about four hours total. Plenty of YouTube guides but the tensioner was trickier than expected.
NASA's latest data from the James Webb telescope suggests the early universe had more mature galaxies than current models predict. Fascinating implications.
If religion provides comfort to people without harming others, why do atheists feel the need to argue against it constantly? Live and let live.
Looking for a good ray tracing library that works well with Python. I've tried PyOpenGL but the performance is poor for complex scenes.
Does anyone have the stats on save percentage for NHL goalies this season? I'm putting together a fantasy hockey spreadsheet.
Interesting study out of Johns Hopkins linking chronic sleep deprivation to increased risk of Alzheimer's. The amyloid clearance mechanism is compelling.
Universal background checks would not prevent law-abiding citizens from owning firearms. The opposition to this is purely ideological, not practical.
Extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence. Belief in miracles fails this basic epistemological standard completely.
The clutch on my VW Golf is slipping badly above 3000 RPM. Mechanic quoted me $800 for a full clutch replacement. Does that sound right?
Mars colonisation is probably fifty years away at minimum. The radiation exposure during transit alone is a massive unsolved problem.
Buddhism offers a path to inner peace without requiring belief in a personal creator God. It's the most philosophically coherent of the major traditions.
Anyone know a good algorithm for anti-aliasing edges in a 2D sprite renderer? MSAA seems like overkill for my use case.
The Bruins power play has been embarrassingly inefficient this season. They need to rethink the entire setup on the man advantage.
My GP prescribed SSRIs for anxiety but I'm worried about dependency. How long do most people stay on them and what is the withdrawal like?
Red flag laws are a dangerous precedent. Confiscating guns based on accusation alone violates due process rights.
The moral framework most atheists operate under is essentially secular humanism. It works perfectly well without any divine grounding.
Bought a used BMW 3 series and the check engine light came on immediately. Ran the OBD-II codes myself: P0171 and P0174, both lean mixture banks.
The Voyager probes have now been travelling for over 45 years. The fact that we can still communicate with them is a remarkable engineering achievement.
The Sermon on the Mount contains some of the most radical ethical teaching in human history regardless of whether you believe Jesus was divine.
I'm working on a shader that simulates subsurface scattering for skin rendering. The current approach is too expensive computationally. Any suggestions?
Penalty shot statistics in the NHL playoffs are fascinating. Home teams convert at a significantly lower rate than in regular season.
The evidence for the gut-brain axis is getting stronger. Microbiome research may fundamentally change how we treat depression and anxiety.
An armed society is a polite society. Crime rates in states with concealed carry are consistently lower than in states with restrictive gun laws.
Religious experience is real as an experience. The question is whether it accurately tracks an external supernatural reality or is a product of the brain.
Synthetic engine oils vs conventional in a high-mileage engine. I know synthetic is better but will switching cause seal issues after 120,000 miles?
Europa almost certainly has a liquid water ocean beneath its ice shell. If there's anywhere in the solar system besides Earth that could have life, it's there.
Islamic theology has a rich philosophical tradition that Western scholars consistently underestimate. Al-Ghazali's critique of Aristotelian philosophy is extraordinary.
Struggling to get correct perspective projection in my homemade renderer. The near and far clipping planes are correct but objects still look distorted.
The trade deadline is going to be interesting. Several top teams are clearly one defenceman away from being genuine Stanley Cup contenders.
Long COVID neurological symptoms are proving very difficult to treat. The mechanism seems to involve persistent inflammation but the specifics are unclear.
The data clearly shows that more guns lead to more gun deaths. This is not a political statement, it is an empirical finding replicated in dozens of studies.
I left Christianity after reading Bart Ehrman's work on the textual inconsistencies in the New Testament. It was a slow and painful process.
Spent the weekend doing a full brake job on my Subaru. New pads, rotors and caliper slides. The difference in braking feel is night and day.
Titan's methane lakes are one of the strangest things in the solar system. A world with liquid on its surface but not water. Incredible.
The concept of grace in Christian theology is genuinely beautiful whether or not you believe in its metaphysical basis.
Does anyone have experience implementing efficient spatial hashing for collision detection in a 2D game engine? Looking for a good bucket size formula.
Ovechkin is the greatest goal scorer in NHL history and the debate is basically over at this point. The records speak for themselves.
Statins have an impressive evidence base for reducing cardiovascular events but the muscle side effects are real and often dismissed by doctors.
Every mass shooting reignites this debate but nothing changes. At what point do we prioritise children's lives over gun rights?
Sam Harris makes compelling arguments but his treatment of Islamic culture is sometimes more polemic than philosophical. Rigour requires consistency.
My Ford F-150's transmission is slipping between 2nd and 3rd. Fluid looks fine. Could this be a solenoid issue or is the clutch pack worn?
The Drake Equation is more useful as a thinking tool than as an actual calculation. Too many unknown variables to produce meaningful numbers.
Evangelical Christianity's influence on American politics has been largely negative. Mixing religion and governance rarely ends well historically.
Working on procedural terrain generation using simplex noise. Getting some tiling artefacts at chunk boundaries that I can't seem to eliminate.
Watching the ice surface at a live NHL game is completely different from TV. You realise just how fast these players are moving.
The link between chronic stress and inflammation is well established. But translating that into practical clinical interventions remains challenging.
Constitutional carry is now the law in over twenty states and the predicted waves of violence have not materialised. The data matters.
The problem of evil remains the most compelling argument against the existence of an omnipotent, omniscient and benevolent God.
Anyone know if you can safely run 91 octane in an engine rated for 87? I know about the knock sensor adjusting timing but is there any actual risk?
Kepler data suggests there could be billions of Earth-like planets in the habitable zone of Sun-like stars just in our galaxy alone.
The mystical traditions within all major religions converge on remarkably similar experiences of unity and transcendence. That overlap is hard to dismiss.
I'm implementing a particle system for fire simulation. Getting the right colour temperature gradient is surprisingly tricky. Tips welcome.
The goalie interference rules in the NHL are inconsistently enforced to the point of being completely meaningless. Needs a complete overhaul.
New research suggests aspirin's benefits for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease no longer outweigh the bleeding risks. Guidance is changing.
Firearms are the most effective equaliser available to people who cannot physically defend themselves. That is the practical argument for gun rights.
I find the framing of atheism as requiring courage or bravery somewhat self-congratulatory. In most Western countries it carries minimal social cost.
Finally tracked down a mystery rattle on my Mazda to a heat shield on the exhaust. Twenty cents of bolts and the problem is completely solved.
The atmospheric composition data from JWST on exoplanet atmospheres is going to revolutionise our understanding of planetary formation.
Liberation theology's emphasis on the poor and marginalised is one of the most genuinely radical traditions in modern religious thought.
What's the most efficient data structure for a tile-based game engine? Currently using a flat array but cache performance feels like it could be better.
The salary cap situation for several original six teams is going to make the next off-season genuinely fascinating from a management perspective.
Immunotherapy has transformed outcomes for some cancers but the response rates vary enormously and we still don't fully understand why.
The gun lobby's influence over elected officials is a direct result of campaign finance laws that allow unlimited dark money into politics.
Christopher Hitchens was entertaining but often sacrificed accuracy for wit. His brother Peter's religious conversion makes for a much more interesting read.
Replaced the front wheel bearings on my Civic myself. Took about three hours with a hydraulic press. Would have been $400 at the dealer.
The question of whether Pluto should be a planet is genuinely interesting from a classification standpoint. The IAU definition has real problems.
The Quaker tradition of silent worship and direct inner experience of the divine is deeply appealing to me even though I'm largely agnostic.
Normal mapping and bump mapping are very different techniques that people constantly conflate. One modifies geometry, the other only affects lighting.
Minor league hockey is genuinely excellent entertainment. The pace is often faster than the NHL and the tickets are a fraction of the price.
The opioid crisis emerged partly because pharmaceutical companies actively misrepresented the addiction potential of oxycodone. Criminal negligence.
Gun buyback programmes have negligible impact on crime because criminals don't surrender their illegal weapons. Well-intentioned but ineffective policy.
The fine-tuning argument for God's existence is more interesting than most atheists admit. The anthropic principle only partially addresses it.
Anyone have experience swapping a manual gearbox into a car that came standard with an automatic? Interested in what the wiring challenges look like.
Wormholes are theoretically permitted by general relativity but the energy requirements to keep one open would be physically unachievable.
The prosperity gospel is a genuine theological aberration that has caused enormous harm to vulnerable people. It deserves sharp criticism.
I'm building a voxel engine and struggling with ambient occlusion. The naive approach works but is far too slow for large scenes.
The expansion draft mechanics in the NHL create genuinely interesting strategic decisions for general managers protecting their rosters.
Antibiotic resistance is one of the most serious public health challenges of the next century. The pipeline of new antibiotics is almost empty.
Mandatory storage laws for firearms make sense from a safety perspective and do not infringe on the right to own a gun.
I was raised Catholic but left the church in my twenties. I still find the ritual and community aspects valuable even without the metaphysical commitments.
High mileage oil additives are largely marketing. A good quality full synthetic is all you need in any engine, high mileage or not.
The Hubble constant tension between early and late universe measurements is either a systematic error or genuinely new physics. Exciting either way.
Interfaith dialogue only works when participants are genuinely open to being changed by the encounter. Most interfaith events are just parallel monologues.
Screen space ambient occlusion is cheap but has obvious artefacts. RTAO gives much better results at a steep performance cost. Tradeoffs everywhere.
The economic impact of a successful Stanley Cup run on a host city is significant. Hotels, restaurants and retail all see measurable uplifts.
CAR-T cell therapy represents a genuine paradigm shift in cancer treatment. We're only at the beginning of understanding its full potential.
The argument that more guns make us safer is empirically testable and the evidence from international comparisons does not support it.
Secular ethics does not require grounding in divine command theory. Contractualism, consequentialism and virtue ethics all provide coherent alternatives.
Anyone else notice that modern cars with electric power steering feel completely numb compared to older hydraulic systems? I miss the road feel.
The search for Planet Nine in the outer solar system is based on gravitational anomalies in trans-Neptunian object orbits. Still unconfirmed.
The ethical teachings of Jesus, regardless of one's theology, represent one of the most demanding moral programmes ever articulated.
Level of detail systems for terrain rendering are still a solved problem with geomipmapping. LOD transitions without popping remain the hard part.
An expansion team in a non-traditional hockey market is only viable long-term if the youth development infrastructure is built from scratch early.
The evidence base for acupuncture beyond placebo is weak at best. Yet it remains widely reimbursed by insurance. Follow the evidence, not tradition.
The founders debated the militia clause for days. Pretending the Second Amendment has one obvious meaning ignores the actual historical record.
Dawkins is a brilliant biologist but his philosophical arguments are surprisingly crude for someone so intellectually confident.
